title: scirpus compressus subsp. bonannii
intro: Scirpus compressus subsp. bonannii, also known as Bonann's sedge, is a plant species belonging to the Cyperaceae family. It is a perennial, wetland plant native to Europe and North Africa. Bonann's sedge has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to promote health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing skin irritations.
